title: RL Foundations
learningObjectives: Define agents, environments, states, observations, action spaces, policies, and trajectories and explain how they interact in the RL loop.
Distinguish deterministic and stochastic policies and implement both in PyTorch using categorical and diagonal Gaussian distributions.
Compute finite-horizon undiscounted and infinite-horizon discounted returns, and explain when each formulation is appropriate.
Define V^π, Q^π, and A^π and derive the Bellman equations that connect them.
Classify RL algorithms along the model-free/model-based and policy-optimization/Q-learning axes and name key tradeoffs.
Derive the basic policy gradient from the log-derivative trick and explain why reward-to-go reduces variance.

readings.title: Before You Start: Prerequisites & Learning Path
RL Fundamentals: Agents, Environments, and Policies
